extraction.one_line_summary: BARGUIL Warren soloed to victory from a 12-rider breakaway after EVENEPOEL Remco and others missed a crucial corner while chasing.
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates refused to chase alone with Thomas 1:08 down overall, allowing 4-minute gap before other teams contributed.
EVENEPOEL Remco attacked with POGACAR on circuit climbs but missed corner, losing momentum while POGACAR recovered faster.
BARGUIL timed final attack on last circuit climb series, hitting cobbled section first to establish winning 51-second gap.
BRAMBILLA dropped from breakaway then rejoined chase group, exploiting positioning between break and peloton during corner chaos.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: missed crucial corner worse than others in same incident, couldn't recover while POGACAR did
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: turned quickest after missing corner and regained position, showing superior bike handling under pressure
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: withheld effort while ROUSSEAU Clément worked, then soloed with perfect timing on final climbs
rider_signals.THOMAS Benjamin: neutral: 1:08 deficit caused UAE to ease pace but couldn't follow BARGUIL's final acceleration
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: described as lively on punishing uncategorized climbs, sitting 6th at 1:00 despite age
